_comment: Canonical example for the RISKS block emitted by the solution-architect inside outputs/phase-2-architecture.md. Two arrays: `risks[]` (narrative — risk + mitigation pairs the user reviews at the Phase 2 gate) and `deferred_items[]` (the structured INDEX of items that will NOT ship in the minimum slice — each one maps to a `tier: D` sub-task in TASK_SKELETON). Phase 4.5's task-planner reads `outputs/blocks/risks.json` to populate per-repo Out-of-Scope sections deterministically — replacing the prior prose-tag scan (`deferred` / `out of scope` / `follow-up` / `v2` / `enhancement` keywords). Each `deferred_items[].id` is the stable address the planner uses in `deferral_reason: "RISKS DEF-N — {rationale}"` against TASK_SKELETON `tier: D` entries. Schema reference and consumer list live in block-schemas.md. Strip the _comment field from real output.

risks.summary: Bulk endpoint may saturate the S3 client when a 50-file batch arrives all at once
risks.mitigation: Cap concurrent uploads to 10 per batch; circuit-break on S3 5xx with exponential backoff

risks.summary: Search-svc indexing may lag if `book.uploaded` events queue faster than the consumer drains
risks.mitigation: Monitor consumer lag; alert at >60s; existing autoscaling on the consumer keeps lag bounded under projected load

deferred_items.summary: Auto-retry of failed files in a bulk batch
deferred_items.rationale: User can manually re-attempt via the UI in v1; auto-retry adds idempotency + dedup complexity not worth the effort for the initial release

deferred_items.summary: Bulk delete and bulk status-change endpoints
deferred_items.rationale: Single-book delete and status-change already work; bulk variants can be added once user demand is observed

deferred_items.summary: Background scheduling of bulk uploads via a worker queue
deferred_items.rationale: v1 ships a synchronous-respond endpoint; queue-based dispatch is a different architecture decision deferred to a future RFC
